description:
# Heuristic-and-emission flip; pure CLIF improvement

## What changes

In `lower_pattern_matrix`'s Decision::Switch handler:
- if cases.len() >= 3 AND keys are same kind AND key spread <= 64,
  emit `Term::Switch` directly
- else emit chained `Prim::TypeTest + Term::If` (today's behavior).

That's it. The IR primitive landed in .E.1 with full test coverage.

## What stays

- The chained fallback (correct for sparse / small-N cases).
- All matrix logic from .D.

## What's removed

- Chained `If(BinOp::Eq, ...)` for dense atom/int dispatch — replaced
  by single `Term::Switch`.

## Acceptance

- Any fixture with ≥3-way atom or int dispatch: golden re-blessed,
  strictly smaller (single Switch vs chained Ifs).
- ast_eval has only 2-arity-groups so doesn't trigger this — but a
  multi-clause fn with 3+ same-arity clauses (or 3+ atom-dispatching
  case clauses) does.
- New fixture (or extend existing) covering ≥3-way atom dispatch to
  exercise the Switch emission path.
- All other fixtures unchanged.
- Three-path parity.

## Risk

Threshold tuning. Pick N=3, spread≤64 as starting point; revisit only
if a real fixture shows degradation (unlikely — br_table on 3-4 keys is
already cheaper than 3-4 sequential compare-and-branch).

## Why after D

D establishes the matrix; E.2 is a peephole on top of it. Separating
keeps each ticket reviewable.

comments.text: DEFERRED post-research; depends on .43.4 being un-deferred. See .43.5 for rationale.

title: fz-ul4.43.E.2: matrix emits Term::Switch when dense
